mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which compounds corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings forecasts and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in over 60% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as markets price in sustained earnings growth.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns with Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 240.0, persistent inflation forces monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 240.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of triggered months, invalidating the earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a manufacturing contraction phase signal weakening supply-side momentum that immediately triggers equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when INDPRO declines during contraction phases.
